data.title: [commit-workflow]: review sub-agent writes artifacts to wrong ARTIFACTS_DIR in worktree sessions
data.description:
## Summary
When dispatching code-reviewer sub-agents from a worktree session, if WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR is not passed explicitly, the sub-agent computes its own ARTIFACTS_DIR from its CWD. In worktrees, the CWD-based hash differs from the orchestrator session's ARTIFACTS_DIR, causing review artifacts (reviewer-findings.json, review-status) to be written to a different path than where record-review.sh and the review gate expect to find them.

## Reproduction
1. Start a sprint in a worktree session
2. Dispatch a code-reviewer sub-agent without passing WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR
3. Sub-agent writes reviewer-findings.json to /tmp/workflow-plugin-<worktree-hash>/
4. Orchestrator's record-review.sh reads from /tmp/workflow-plugin-<main-session-hash>/ — finds no file

## Expected Behavior
Sub-agent should write to the same ARTIFACTS_DIR as the orchestrator.

## Actual Behavior
Sub-agent computes a different ARTIFACTS_DIR from its CWD, causing review status to be missing or stale.

## Workaround
Pass WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR=<path> explicitly in every code-reviewer sub-agent dispatch from commit/review workflows.
